name: Kronecker Sequence (Golden Ratio)
idea: Rotation angles from additive recurrence with golden ratio fractional part: angle_i = (i * phi_frac) mod 1 * 2pi
novelty: Kronecker/additive recurrence with the golden ratio produces the theoretically optimal 1D equidistributed sequence (three-distance theorem), different from golden angle multiplication

name: Weyl Sequence (sqrt(2)) Rotations
idea: Equidistributed angles via Weyl sequence: angle_i = (i * sqrt(2)) mod 1 * 2pi, guaranteed uniform by Weyl equidistribution theorem
novelty: Applies Weyl equidistribution theorem with sqrt(2) as irrational base - provably equidistributed but with different correlation structure than golden-ratio sequences

name: Rotation + Shear Transformation
idea: Combine Halton-sequence rotations with small deterministic shear transforms (affine, non-rigid) for richer geometric diversity
novelty: Goes beyond pure rotations by adding shear (non-orthogonal affine transform), breaking the rotation-only paradigm while preserving topology

name: Power-Law Angle Distribution
idea: Non-uniform angular sampling with power-law concentration near cardinal directions (0/90/180/270 degrees) where dihedral transforms are sparse
novelty: Information-theoretic approach: allocates more augmentation budget near cardinal directions where the 8 dihedrals leave gaps, rather than uniform coverage

name: Halton Base-3 Rotations
idea: Halton sequence with base 3 for quasi-random angles, producing fundamentally different low-discrepancy distribution than base 2
novelty: Different prime base produces different digit-reversal pattern, offering complementary angle distribution that fills gaps left by base-2 Halton

name: VDC-Dihedral Expansion
idea: Van der Corput sequence angles with 2x expansion: each rotation produces both the rotated copy and its transposed (x,y)->(y,x) variant
novelty: Combines quasi-random angles with axis-swap augmentation, doubling effective diversity per rotation angle through orthogonal coordinate permutation

name: Halton Base-2 Rotations
idea: Use Halton quasi-random sequence (base 2) for rotation angles, providing low-discrepancy angular coverage that outperforms golden angle spacing
novelty: Applies number-theoretic Halton sequence to TSP augmentation - the low-discrepancy property guarantees angles are more uniformly spread than any periodic sequence

name: Prime-Sqrt Multi-Base Rotations
idea: Cycle through sqrt of prime numbers (sqrt(2), sqrt(3), sqrt(5)...) as irrational multipliers for angle generation
novelty: Multi-base irrational rotation - instead of single irrational number, cycles through 15 different irrational bases to maximize angular decorrelation across copies

name: Interleaved Halton Base-2&3
idea: Alternate between Halton base-2 and base-3 sequences for every other augmentation copy, combining two quasi-random distributions
novelty: Interleaving two different low-discrepancy sequences creates a hybrid distribution with better space-filling properties than either alone

name: Rotation + Anisotropic Scaling
idea: Halton-sequence rotations combined with mild anisotropic x-axis scaling (0.9-1.1x) before normalization, breaking pure rotation symmetry
novelty: Introduces controlled aspect-ratio distortion alongside rotation, allowing the model to see the instance at slightly different aspect ratios which reveals different greedy path structures
